title: Free Will Hunting

jokes.text: Decisions, decisions. Should I wear my nerd glasses today, or not? Usually I don't. Whatever. It doesn't really matter.
jokes.setup: Fry casually dismisses the importance of his decision
jokes.punchline: The Professor immediately contradicts him with philosophical importance
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's theme while showing Fry's characteristic obliviousness contrasted with Professor's overwrought philosophizing

jokes.text: Ah, who asked you?
jokes.setup: Fry asks for advice about his decision
jokes.punchline: Gets annoyed when someone actually gives him advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - asks for input then rejects it, very economical character beat

jokes.text: Ooh, I like your nerd glasses. Are you a college student?
jokes.setup: Recruiter notices the glasses and assumes Fry is intellectual
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's just a shallow recruitment tactic
jokes.explanation: Decent setup for college recruitment scam premise

jokes.text: Yes, I'd like to enroll in college? You're still talking to me. Oops.
jokes.setup: Fry enthusiastically agrees to enroll
jokes.punchline: Recruiter realizes he's made a mistake and awkwardly tries to escape
jokes.explanation: Good awkward comedy showing how recruitment scams work and Fry's naivety

jokes.text: $10,000 tuition? I can't afford that!
jokes.setup: Bender is shocked by the high tuition cost
jokes.punchline: Sets up the loan shark introduction
jokes.explanation: Functional setup line, not particularly funny on its own

jokes.text: Psst. Over there. Where? I mean over here. Sorry, I forgot where I was.
jokes.setup: Trying to be mysterious and direct Bender somewhere
jokes.punchline: Gets confused about his own location
jokes.explanation: Nice bit of bumbling criminal comedy, economical execution

jokes.text: We, the robot mafia, are prepared to lend you $10,000 at an interest rate of 10,000%. That's easy to remember.
jokes.setup: Robot mafia offers loan with outrageous interest rate
jokes.punchline: Bender focuses on the memorability rather than the obvious scam
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bender logic - completely missing the point of a 10,000% interest rate. Great character integration and absurdist math humor.

jokes.text: Yo hombre, you think you're cool enough to sit way in back with us college toughs? Yes, I do. Yo, in that case you're all right, man.
jokes.setup: Tough guy challenges Bender's coolness
jokes.punchline: Accepts Bender immediately based on confidence alone
jokes.explanation: Good satire of how easy it is to get accepted into 'tough' groups

jokes.text: Mr. Bender, I realize class has only been in session for 32 seconds, but you've fallen in with a bad crowd. I know, right?
jokes.setup: Professor notes how quickly Bender got into trouble
jokes.punchline: Bender is proud of his rapid corruption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender reaction - takes pride in being told he's bad. The 32 seconds detail adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: Well, I got my tuition back, minus $9,000 for the damage I caused to the dean's wife.
jokes.setup: Bender got his tuition back
jokes.punchline: Reveals he caused $9,000 in damage to someone's wife
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect misdirection. The casual way he mentions damaging a person is very Bender.

jokes.text: Hey, what's that? Is it drugs? Nah, it ain't drugs. But it's a lot like drugs.
jokes.setup: Bender asks about suspicious substance
jokes.punchline: Not technically drugs but functionally identical
jokes.explanation: Good satire of drug euphemisms and legal loopholes

jokes.text: Never heard of it. Sure.
jokes.setup: Bender admits he's never heard of 'spark'
jokes.punchline: Immediately agrees to try it anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender response - maximum poor decision making in minimum words

jokes.text: You like it? Eh, I can take it or leave it. I guess I'll take it.
jokes.setup: Bender claims indifference to the substance
jokes.punchline: Immediately decides to take more
jokes.explanation: Classic addiction humor showing the lie of 'I can quit anytime'. Good character work for Bender.

jokes.text: I'll take that big purple dinosaur, please. On the face.
jokes.setup: Bender choosing a tough gang tattoo
jokes.punchline: Chooses a childish purple dinosaur for his face
jokes.explanation: Perfectly subverts expectations of tough gang imagery. Very Bender to choose something completely inappropriate.

jokes.text: We have a rival gang? I hate them!
jokes.setup: Gang member mentions rival gang
jokes.punchline: Bender instantly develops hatred for people he just learned exist
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy and perfect Bender character work. Shows how quickly he adopts tribal mentality without any actual reason.

jokes.text: Phew! That was close, eh, Fabricio?
jokes.setup: Bender thinks they escaped danger
jokes.punchline: Fabricio is actually dead (revealed in next scene)
jokes.explanation: Dark humor setup - Bender's relief contrasts with the actual tragedy that follows

jokes.text: Oh, nothing sordid, I assure you! Simply vomit on me, ever so gently, while I humiliate a pheasant.
jokes.setup: Client assures Bender the job isn't sordid
jokes.punchline: Describes incredibly weird and specific fetish
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of 'nothing sordid' with incredibly specific and bizarre request. The pheasant detail makes it memorable.

jokes.text: Save it for the boudoir!
jokes.setup: Client describes bizarre fetish
jokes.punchline: Bender's prudish reaction using fancy French word
jokes.explanation: Good contrast - Bender acting prim about something relatively tame compared to his usual behavior

jokes.text: Still, to have your own pool...
jokes.setup: Bender describes hitting rock bottom in blood and urine
jokes.punchline: Finds the silver lining of pool ownership
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Bender humor. Takes the most horrific description and finds a positive spin with perfect comedic timing.

jokes.text: Not guilty! Although I did do it. That's right, I'm bad.
jokes.setup: Bender pleads not guilty to charges
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself and brags about it
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - can't help but brag about his crimes even while trying to avoid punishment

jokes.text: However, my client made no such decisions!
jokes.setup: Lawyer argues Bender made bad decisions
jokes.punchline: Argues he made no decisions at all
jokes.explanation: Sets up the free will defense cleverly, introducing the episode's central philosophical concept

jokes.text: You see, Bender here is what is known in scientific circles as a 'robot'
jokes.setup: Lawyer building to dramatic reveal
jokes.punchline: States the completely obvious fact that Bender is a robot
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan humor - treating obvious information as if it's a revelation

jokes.text: You see, Bender here is what is known in scientific circles as a 'robot'...
jokes.setup: Lawyer builds to dramatic revelation
jokes.punchline: States the completely obvious fact that Bender is a robot
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan humor treating obvious information as revelatory

jokes.text: I'm awake, I'm awake! Order, order!
jokes.setup: Judge was apparently sleeping during proceedings
jokes.punchline: Wakes up and tries to regain authority
jokes.explanation: Standard courtroom comedy beat - judge sleeping during important proceedings

jokes.text: Computation complete. Not guilty!
jokes.setup: Judge considers the free will argument about robots
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's also a robot and his verdict is just programming
jokes.explanation: Excellent philosophical comedy - the robot judge realizes his own lack of free will in the moment of deciding about free will

jokes.text: From now on, no matter what cool crimes I commit, people will say, 'Bender didn't do that! It was just Bender's programming!'
jokes.setup: Bender is upset about the free will ruling
jokes.punchline: He's upset he won't get credit for his crimes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - most people would be happy to avoid responsibility, but he wants credit for being bad

jokes.text: Well, I committed these crimes, do you hear me? I'm guilty! Get that innocent robot out of my courtroom! Guilty!
jokes.setup: Bender insists he's guilty
jokes.punchline: Judge throws him out for being innocent
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal - being thrown out of court for insisting you're guilty

jokes.text: A robot free will unit? Impossible! And even if it were possible, which it's not, it would still be inconceivable... which it is!
jokes.setup: Professor declares something impossible
jokes.punchline: Keeps adding redundant levels of impossibility
jokes.explanation: Good Professor character work - his tendency to over-explain and contradict himself

jokes.text: I wish I was a real boy... Then I'd show them. I'd kill them all.
jokes.setup: Bender references Pinocchio's innocent wish
jokes.punchline: Twists it into mass murder fantasy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of the Pinocchio reference. Takes innocent childhood wish and makes it sinister in perfect Bender fashion.

jokes.text: They're just too dang unpredictable, what with their free will and all. Amen, brother. 'Amen, brother?' Pfft. What a boring, predictable thing to say.
jokes.setup: Robot complains about human unpredictability
jokes.punchline: Bender realizes his own response was completely predictable
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about predictability while demonstrating it. Ties into the episode's free will theme.

jokes.text: Goodbye, sweet meatbags... ...sweetbags.
jokes.setup: Bender tries to say a touching goodbye
jokes.punchline: Corrects himself to remove the 'meat' part
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay joke, shows Bender trying to be inclusive to robots while saying goodbye

jokes.text: Son, philosophy's for thems what don't got to work for a livin'. But me, I got a sick child to feed.
jokes.setup: Bender asks philosophical question
jokes.punchline: Working-class robot dismisses philosophy for practical concerns
jokes.explanation: Good class-based humor, robots having working-class attitudes and family concerns

jokes.text: Son, philosophy's for thems what don't got to work for a livin'. But me, I got a sick child to feed.
jokes.setup: Bender asks philosophical question
jokes.punchline: Robot worker gives blue-collar response about having a sick child
jokes.explanation: Good humor about robots having very human working class concerns

jokes.text: Silence! It pleases the council. Silence! ...Silence! Well? Speak up.
jokes.setup: Council demands silence repeatedly
jokes.punchline: Then immediately demands someone speak
jokes.explanation: Standard bureaucratic absurdity humor - contradictory demands from authority figures

jokes.text: Well? Speak up.
jokes.setup: Elder keeps saying 'Silence!'
jokes.punchline: Then demands someone speak up
jokes.explanation: Simple contradiction humor - demanding speech after demanding silence

jokes.text: So, are we automatons? Yes. But we are magnificent automatons.
jokes.setup: Philosophical question about their nature
jokes.punchline: Accepts the limitation but adds dignity to it
jokes.explanation: Nice philosophical humor that fits the episode theme, finding dignity in determinism

jokes.text: How does a robot join this monk outfit? Just put on this monk outfit.
jokes.setup: Bender asks about complex joining process
jokes.punchline: Literally just put on the clothes
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay on 'outfit' meaning both clothing and organization

jokes.text: I'm 40% empty.
jokes.setup: Monk notes Bender's free will slot is empty
jokes.punchline: Bender uses his catchphrase format in new context
jokes.explanation: Good use of established Bender catchphrase in existential context, connecting to the free will theme

jokes.text: Any of you guys got a spaceship?
jokes.setup: Monk explains Mom's quest to create free will
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately abandons enlightenment for action
jokes.explanation: Good Bender character moment - his spiritual journey ends the moment he has a practical lead

jokes.text: 'Cause I don't have free will. I need you guys to plan the crime and make all the crime decisions.
jokes.setup: Bender recruits crew for heist
jokes.punchline: Uses lack of free will as excuse to make others do the work
jokes.explanation: Clever use of the free will concept - Bender finds a way to exploit his philosophical limitation

jokes.text: How do we murder the guards? Hockey sticks or sharpened footballs?
jokes.setup: Bender asks about murder methods
jokes.punchline: Suggests absurd sports equipment as weapons
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with absurd weapon choices, fits Bender's casual attitude toward violence

jokes.text: I should have paid more attention in kindergarten.
jokes.setup: Bender can't figure out simple shapes puzzle
jokes.punchline: Blames lack of kindergarten attention
jokes.explanation: Good character humor - robot blaming childhood education for simple task failure

jokes.text: Welcome to Momcorp. Did you find everything you were burgling for?
jokes.setup: Standard customer service greeting
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges they're there to steal things
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay on customer service language, Mom's casual acceptance of criminal activity

jokes.text: Then you got surprised by my swivel chair for nothing.
jokes.setup: Mom reveals free will unit doesn't exist
jokes.punchline: References the earlier swivel chair invention request
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to earlier throwaway line, pays off the swivel chair setup

jokes.text: Once we give robots free will, they're sure to rise up and threaten mankind with extermination. ...I'll sell a second wave of robots to protect the terrified survivors of the first wave.
jokes.setup: Mom explains robots will destroy humanity
jokes.punchline: Plans to profit from both the destruction and the protection
jokes.explanation: Great evil business plan humor - creating both the problem and the solution for maximum profit

jokes.text: But-but... it's just a prototype. It's, uh, not fully operational. Yes.
jokes.setup: Professor called a doddering fool
jokes.punchline: Tries to cover up that he actually succeeded
jokes.explanation: Shows Professor's deceptiveness, sets up the reveal that he did create it

jokes.text: To this day, my poor, sweet robots can't exterminate humanity without being told to.
jokes.setup: Mom expresses sadness about her robots
jokes.punchline: She's sad they can't kill people independently
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mom character work - maternal language applied to genocide. Great dark humor.

jokes.text: I hate those!
jokes.setup: Professor mentions soul-searching journey
jokes.punchline: Bender's disgusted reaction to self-reflection
jokes.explanation: Good Bender character moment - his hatred of personal growth and introspection

jokes.text: Kill all humans!
jokes.setup: Professor reveals who has the free will unit
jokes.punchline: Tiny toy Bender demonstrates murderous free will
jokes.explanation: Good use of Bender's catchphrase, irony that the toy has the free will the real Bender wants

jokes.text: A toy Bender had free will, and the real Bender didn't? Pretty cool, eh? It makes you think.
jokes.setup: Professor points out the ironic situation
jokes.punchline: Fry's underwhelmed response about thinking
jokes.explanation: Mild philosophical humor, Fry's typical resistance to deep thinking

jokes.text: I programmed you to be incapable of picking up the free will unit. You precognizant bastard.
jokes.setup: Professor reveals he blocked Bender from taking the unit
jokes.punchline: Bender's creative insult using big word incorrectly
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Bender trying to sound smart with big words but using them wrong

jokes.text: Poor deterministic Bender. I programmed you to be incapable of harming me. Oh, I've never been more sure I was right to deny you free will.
jokes.setup: Professor gloats about his programming
jokes.punchline: Proves his point by Bender's inability to shoot him
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the final twist, Professor's smug satisfaction at being right

jokes.text: Due to the quantum nature of the device, there's no possible way of knowing.
jokes.setup: Bender asks which way is on/off on the free will unit
jokes.punchline: Professor uses quantum physics to avoid giving clear instructions
jokes.explanation: Good use of quantum uncertainty principle for comedy, creates suspense about whether free will is activated

jokes.text: Due to the quantum nature of the device, there's no possible way of knowing.
jokes.setup: Bender asks which way is on/off on the free will switch
jokes.punchline: Professor uses quantum mechanics to avoid answering simple question
jokes.explanation: Good Professor character moment - using complex science to avoid simple questions

jokes.text: I still can't do it. Well, what do you know? I guess you really don't want to shoot me after all. Oop, the safety was on.
jokes.setup: Bender can't shoot, Professor assumes it's because Bender doesn't want to
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was just the safety catch
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and perfect episode-ending twist. Subverts the entire free will theme with a mundane mechanical explanation.

jokes.text: Well, what do you know? I guess you really don't want to shoot me after all. Oop, the safety was on.
jokes.setup: Bender can't shoot, Professor thinks it's because he doesn't want to
jokes.punchline: Gun safety was just on
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - philosophical explanation undercut by mundane mechanical issue

jokes.text: Ah, yeah! Guilty!
jokes.setup: Bender is found guilty of attempted murder
jokes.punchline: He's enthusiastic about being found guilty
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to earlier moment, Bender finally gets the criminal recognition he wanted

tmdb_overview: Bender searches for the meaning of life after learning that robots lack free will.
